title: Multiple Stacks Implementation Strategies
description: A flowchart illustrating two common methods for implementing multiple stacks using a single array, showing memory allocation, overflow, and underflow conditions.
relationships: Fixed Division splits the array into two fixed-size halves, each serving one stack
Flexible Division allows stacks to grow towards each other from opposite ends, dynamically sharing the memory space
Overflow occurs when a stack attempts to push an element beyond its allocated memory space
Underflow occurs when a pop operation is attempted on an empty stack
